item.title: Iran says Hormuz completely closed
item.description: Iran’s new body overseeing the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday confirmed a complete closure order for the strategic waterway until further notice, after the Revolutionary Guards announced the move overnight....

item.title: US refunds $22b in customs duties in May
item.description: The US Treasury Department said Wednesday that authorities made $22 billion in customs refunds last month, marking the first series of repayments after the Supreme Court struck down president Donald Trump’s global tariffs......

item.title: Inflation to be cut to 7.5pc in FY27: Khosru
item.description: Finance minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury on Thursday set a target of reducing inflation to 7.5 per cent in the proposed national budget for the 2026–27 financial year....

item.title: World Bank lowers global growth forecast on Iran war impacts
item.description: The World Bank on Thursday lowered its global growth forecast to its lowest level since the pandemic, warning of the expanding economic impacts of the war in the Middle East on countries around the globe......

item.title: US Treasury vows to tap Iran funds for its damage on Gulf allies
item.description: US treasury secretary Scott Bessent vowed Thursday to use Iranian funds to pay for damages that the country causes on Gulf allies, warning of sharp economic consequences from Tehran’s attacks......

item.title: Govt reintroduces provision to legalise undisclosed money
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government on Thursday reintroduced a provision allowing taxpayers to legalise undisclosed income through voluntary disclosure in the proposed national budget for the 2026–27 financial year....

item.title: Sonali Bank sub-branch opens at Metrorail Bhaban
item.description: Sonali Bank PLC started a new sub-branch named ‘Metrorail Bhaban sub-branch, Uttara, Dhaka’ under supervision of Uttara Janapath branch in the capital on Thursday, said a press release......

item.title: TIB seeks asset disclosure of public servants for new pay scale
item.description: Transparency International Bangladesh on Thursday demanded mandatory asset declarations for public servants as a condition for receiving benefits under the proposed new pay scale....

item.title: UCB holds 43rd AGM
item.description: United Commercial Bank PLC on Thursday held its 43rd annual general meeting held at Kurmitola Golf Club in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: JU student assaulted in Netrakona ‘for joining’ July uprising
item.description: A second-year student of Jahangirnagar University was allegedly assaulted, extorted and forced to apologise in a video recorded by unidentified attackers in his home district of Netrakona on Wednesday, reportedly for participating in the 2024 mass uprising that ousted the Sheikh Hasina-led Awami League regime from power....

item.title: RupaliCash, Akij Online sign agreement
item.description: Rupali Bank PLC’s mobile financial service RupaliCash and Akij Online signed an agreement on Thursday at the bank’s head office at Dilkusha in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: CURBING VIOLENCE AGAINST WOMEN, CHILDREN: Budget allocations raised
item.description: Allocations for children and women have been increased in the proposed budget for the 2026–27 financial year with particular focus on strengthening the capacity to investigate and respond to incidents of violence....

item.title: BB recognises Karmasangsthan Bank for job creation
item.description: Karmasangsthan Bank has received special recognition from the Bangladesh Bank for its contribution to employment creation through the Supporting Post Covid-19 Small Scale Employment Creation Project (SPCSSECP), funded by the Asian Development Bank and implemented under the supervision of the Bangladesh Bank......

item.title: IBFB marks 20th anniversary
item.description: The International Business Forum of Bangladesh on Thursday marked its 20th anniversary at the Radisson Blu Water Garden Hotel in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: Sundarban Coast Guard station attacked, members injured
item.description: Unidentified people attacked and vandalised the Bangladesh Coast Guard station Harbaria in the Joymonir Ghol area under Mongla in Bagerhat on Thursday....

item.title: Pubali Bank holds 2nd Business Conference
item.description: Pubali Bank PLC’s 2nd Business Conference-2026 of Regional and Corporate Branch Managers was held at bank’s head office auditorium on Thursday, said a press release......

item.title: Prime Bank becomes PCAF signatory
item.description: Prime Bank PLC has officially joined the Partnership for Carbon Accounting Financials, PCAF, becoming one of more than 700 global signatories committed to measuring and disclosing greenhouse gas emissions associated with financial activities, said a press release......

item.title: HC bench to hear 20 death references from Sunday
item.description: A dedicated High Court bench formed to expedite the disposal of cases under the Women and Children Repression Prevention Act has initially listed 20 death reference cases for hearing from Sunday, including the widely discussed rape and murder of an eight-year-old girl in Magura and the 2019 killing of Feni madrassah student Nusrat Jahan Rafi....

item.title: SpaceX on cusp of record IPO that could make Musk a trillionaire
item.description: SpaceX enters the final stretch Thursday before its expected trading on Wall Street as part of the biggest initial public offering in history, which could propel co-founder Elon Musk to trillionaire status......

item.title: Essentials, healthcare become cheaper, imported food, vehicle, tobacco cost more
item.description: The proposed national budget for the financial year 2026-27 recommended sweeping tax reductions on daily essentials, medical devices, digital technology, electric vehicles, and a broad range of industrial inputs....

item.title: Banks, capital market, ICT among 10 priority areas in new budget
item.description: The government has outlined 10 priority sectors in the budget for the financial year 2026–27, aiming to achieve inclusive development and accelerate economic growth to 6.5 per cent....

item.title: Shakira, Burna Boy warm up fans in World Cup opening ceremony
item.description: Shakira and Nigerian singer Burna Boy had the spectators out of their seats in the opening ceremony for the 2026 World Cup in the Estadio Azteca on Thursday ahead of the opening match between co-hosts Mexico and South Africa....

item.title: Partisan interests mustn’t influence bank affairs
item.description: UNREST at Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC warrants an immediate resolution not only for the sake of the bank but also of the banking sector. What is concerning is that the unrest appears to be driven more by partisan political interests than by banking considerations. The Association of Bankers, after a meeting with the Bangladesh Bank governor on June 10, expressed deep......

item.title: Opposition parties term FY27 budget anti-people
item.description: Opposition political parties, including Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami, on Thursday rejected the proposed national budget, describing it as unrealistic, anti-people and likely to burden public with higher taxes....

item.title: Railway burdened by longstanding neglect
item.description: THE derailment of the power car of a passenger train on the Dhaka–Jamalpur route in Mymensingh on June 10, followed by the derailment of a relief train sent for recovery on the same track, exposes a disturbing level of vulnerability in railway infrastructure. The fact that both incidents occurred within hours on the same section, disrupting train communications for around......

item.title: Budget ambitious amid challenges: economists
item.description: Economists have described the proposed Tk 9.38 lakh crore national budget for financial year 2026-27 as ambitious, warning that weak revenue collection, implementation challenges, persistent inflation and structural weaknesses could undermine its growth targets....

item.title: Big budget but why the gloom
item.description: The budget is big. But the gloom that hangs over it is bigger. It is not that the budget is not well-meaning and lacks aspiration. Far from it. It has got all indications of public welfare. Tk 3,00,000 crore development budget speaks for itself. Corporate taxes have been kept unchanged or reduced in some cases....

item.title: Impunity challenged in the plains, tolerated in the hills?
item.description: Thirty years after the abduction of Kalpana Chakma, the burden of waiting for justice rests heavily on her brother, Kalindi Kumar Chakma, yet the memories of that night remain vivid and haunting. Memory has deepened its hold. He recalls the approaching footsteps outside their mud hut, the shades of darkness by the well where they were bound and blindfolded, the......

item.title: Health sector gets biggest budget hike
item.description: The government on Thursday proposed an allocation of Tk 69,409 crore for the health sector in the 2026–27 financial year, accounting for 7.4 per cent of the national budget and marking a 95.6 per cent increase from the revised allocation for the outgoing fiscal year....

item.title: Budget sparks renewable hopes, leaves fossil fuel expansion intact
item.description: The new budget has outlined a strategy for the power and energy sector that appears to have pleased both renewable energy campaigners and fossil fuel businesses in a rare convergence of interests....

item.title: Building knowledge economy
item.description: BANGLADESH’S economy rests, still, on two pillars planted in an earlier era: low-cost apparel exports and remittances from migrant labourers. Both are under existential pressure. Automation reshapes global manufacturing. Artificial intelligence begins to displace the kind of routine processing work that millions of Bangladeshi workers abroad perform. Meanwhile......

item.title: UNGA presidency win and diplomatic context
item.description: THE election of the president of the forthcoming session of the United Nations General Assembly marks a moment that goes beyond procedural routine and enters the realm of symbolic geopolitical signalling. In a closely contested vote, Bangladesh’s candidate — its senior diplomat and foreign minister — secured the position against Cyprus, concluding what has been......

item.title: Mexico beat South Africa to kick off World Cup
item.description: Co-hosts Mexico beat South Africa 2-0 in the opening game of the World Cup at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City on Thursday....

item.title: New Spurs man Senesi called up to Argentina WC squad
item.description: New Tottenham Hotspur signing Marcos Senesi completed a dream 24 hours when on Thursday he was called up to the Argentina World Cup squad to replacing the injured Leonardo Balerdi....

item.title: Yamal, Williams train ahead of Spain's World Cup opener
item.description: Spain stars Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams took part in training on Thursday, allaying fears over their fitness ahead of La Roja's World Cup opener against Cape Verde. ...

item.title: Alisson unfazed by doubts over Brazil heading into World Cup
item.description: Brazil goalkeeper Alisson Becker insisted Thursday that doubts about the record five-time champions could work in the team's favour at the World Cup as they prepare to get their campaign in North America up and running....

item.title: Italian football fans back Spain and Ancelotti's Brazil in Azzurri's absence
item.description:
Deprived of the chance to watch their country at a World Cup for the third successive time, Italy's football fans are eyeing Spain and Carlo Ancelotti's Brazil as teams to support in this year's tournament which starts on Thursday.
...

item.title: Mourinho named Real Madrid coach
item.description:
Real Madrid appointed Jose Mourinho as their coach on Thursday on a three-year deal, with the Portuguese veteran making a sensational return to the Spanish capital after 13 years away.
...

item.title: Shamim Ara takes charge as Rajshahi edn board new chair
item.description: Professor Shamim Ara Chowdhury took charge as new chairperson of the Board of Intermediate and Secondary Education, Rajshahi on Wednesday....

item.title: UCBD, Runner sign memo to boost industry-academia collaboration
item.description: Universal College Bangladesh has recently signed an agreement with Runner Group to give students real-world industry experience and stronger career opportunities....

item.title: JCD activists allegedly beat up Habiganj SAD leader
item.description: Habiganj district unit member secretary of the Student Against Discrimination, Mahdi Hasan, on Thursday was beaten up allegedly by leaders and activists of Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, student wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party....

item.title: BGB-BSF DG-level talks end without joint statement
item.description: The 57th four-day director general-level talks between the Border Guard Bangladesh and the Indian Border Security Force ended on Thursday in New Delhi without issuing a joint statement on the day....

item.title: 24 arrested over attack on police during anti-drug raid at Savar
item.description: The police have arrested 24 people, including five named, in a case over an attack on the law enforcement officers, during a special operation in the Bede Palli area of Savar in Dhaka....

item.title: BMU observes Global Fatty Liver Day 2026
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University observed Global Fatty Liver Day 2026 at BMU in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: BUFT concludes 2-day awareness prog on World Environment Day
item.description: The BGMEA University of Fashion and Technology concluded its two-day programme on World Environment Day, promoting environmental awareness, sustainability and climate action, at BUFT in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: DU holds meeting with ACU to strengthen research collaboration
item.description: The University of Dhaka held a meeting with the Association of Commonwealth Universities to strengthen research collaboration at the Nabab Nawab Ali Chowdhury Senate Bhaban of the university on Thursday....

item.title: Safiqul Islam joins CU as pro-VC
item.description: Professor Md Safiqul Islam has been appointed as pro-vice-chancellor, administration, of Chittagong University....

item.title: Govt sets GDP growth target at 6.5pc for FY27
item.description: The government has set the gross domestic product growth target at 6.5 per cent for the 2026-2027 financial year......

item.title: Eight-day theatre festival begins today at BSA
item.description: An eight-day theatre festival, organised by Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy’s Department of Drama and Film, begins today at the Experimental Theatre Hall of the National Theatre Auditorium in the capital....

item.title: Businesses welcome tax relief, warn of implementation gaps
item.description: Bangladesh’s major business associations and trade chambers broadly described the FY2026-27 budget as business and investment-friendly, welcoming a sweeping package of withholding tax reductions and compliance reforms......

item.title: Budget proposes Tk 826cr for cultural ministry
item.description: The government has proposed an allocation of Tk 826 crore for the ministry of cultural affairs in the national budget for the 2026-27 financial year, up Tk 2 crore from the revised allocation of Tk 824 crore for the current financial year....

item.title: Tk 33,240 crore allocated for banking reforms
item.description: The government has allocated Tk 33,240 crore to continue bank merger and restructuring activities alongside other reform efforts for the 2026-27 financial year......

item.title: Forcibly displaced population drops to 118m in 2025: UN
item.description: The number of forcibly displaced people dropped for the first time in a decade last year, as more opted to return home despite often unsafe and unstable conditions, the UN said Thursday....

item.title: Defence, home get higher allocations
item.description: Allocations for the defence and home ministries in the proposed budget have increased compared with those of the outgoing financial year’s revised budget....

item.title: All export sectors to get bond facilities
item.description: The government announced plans to extend customs bond facilities to all export-oriented sectors beyond readymade garments in the proposed budget for the 2026-27 financial year......

item.title: 6 more testify in Ctg child rape case
item.description: The Chattogram Metropolitan Children Violence Prevention Tribunal on Thursday recorded the testimonies of six more prosecution witnesses in the case over the rape of a four-year-old girl in the port city’s Bakalia area....

item.title: Govt to provide 5-yr tax waiver for semiconductor industry
item.description: Finance minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury on Thursday proposed a five-year tax exemption until June 30, 2031, for the country’s emerging semiconductor industry, aiming to attract investment, create high-skilled jobs and position the country as a player in the global technology value chain....

item.title: Deposit up to Tk 4 lakh to get duty exemption
item.description: The government has proposed raising the excise duty exemption ceiling on bank deposits to Tk 4 lakh from Tk 3 lakh for the 2026-27 fiscal year......

item.title: Tax-free income ceiling to go up in phases
item.description: The government has proposed a phased increase in the tax-free income threshold for individual taxpayers in the proposed national budget of the 2026-27 financial year, alongside enhanced exemptions for women, senior citizens and other vulnerable groups......

item.title: Shopping malls, markets to stay open till 9:00pm
item.description: The government has decided to allow shopping malls, markets and retail shops across the country to remain open until 9pm from June 12, easing restrictions imposed earlier as part of power-saving measures......

item.title: 30 years on, no justice for Kalpana Chakma
item.description: Thirty years after the alleged abduction of Hill Women’s Federation organising secretary Kalpana Chakma from her home in Baghaichari of Rangamati, her family is still waiting for justice and answers about her fate....

item.title: Govt plans capital market reforms to relieve banks
item.description: The government has proposed strengthening Bangladesh’s capital market and expanding alternative financing channels as part of its strategy to reduce the economy’s heavy reliance on bank loans and support long-term investment......
